_meta.description: Manually curated drug interaction rules for the African clinical context. Prioritises HIV/ARV, malaria, TB, and NSAID interactions common in sub-Saharan African healthcare settings.
sources: WHO Guidelines for Treatment of Malaria 3rd Edition
_meta.note: This file is checked FIRST by risk_engine.py before any external API lookup. All rules here are validated for the West African clinical context.

3.label: high
3.action: Strong caution — clinician must review before dispensing

4.label: critical
4.action: MANDATORY human review — do not dispense without senior clinician sign-off

interaction_rules.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit prostaglandin synthesis, reducing renal blood flow and GFR. In patients with existing renal impairment, this can precipitate acute kidney injury or accelerate chronic kidney disease progression.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: Acute kidney injury, worsening of chronic kidney disease, fluid retention, hypertension, hyperkalaemia
interaction_rules.warning_message: CRITICAL: NSAIDs are contraindicated in patients with kidney disease. Ibuprofen and other NSAIDs reduce blood flow to the kidneys and can cause acute kidney failure in this patient.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Avoid all NSAIDs. Use paracetamol (acetaminophen) for pain/fever. Refer to clinician for renal-safe analgesic plan.

interaction_rules.mechanism: NSAIDs cause sodium and water retention and blunt the effect of antihypertensive medications, raising blood pressure.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: Blood pressure elevation, reduced efficacy of antihypertensives, increased cardiovascular event risk
interaction_rules.warning_message: HIGH RISK: NSAIDs reduce the effectiveness of blood pressure medications and can cause dangerous blood pressure spikes in hypertensive patients.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Use paracetamol instead. If NSAID is essential, use lowest dose for shortest duration and monitor BP closely.

interaction_rules.mechanism: Efavirenz is a potent inducer of CYP3A4 and CYP2B6. Artemether and lumefantrine are both metabolised by CYP3A4, leading to significantly reduced plasma concentrations of the antimalarials — up to 75% reduction in lumefantrine exposure.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: Subtherapeutic antimalarial levels, treatment failure, risk of promoting artemisinin resistance, recrudescent malaria
interaction_rules.warning_message: CRITICAL: Efavirenz drastically reduces blood levels of artemether-lumefantrine. This combination risks malaria treatment failure and may promote drug resistance.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Switch to artesunate-amodiaquine if possible, or use double-dose artemether-lumefantrine (4 tablets twice daily for adults) per WHO malaria/HIV co-infection guidelines. Refer to senior clinician.
references: WHO Guidelines for Treatment of Malaria 3rd Ed, Table 9.2

interaction_rules.mechanism: Nevirapine induces CYP3A4. Amodiaquine is metabolised via CYP2C8 to its active metabolite desethylamodiaquine. Combined use has caused severe hepatotoxicity in clinical studies — a trial was stopped prematurely due to dramatic elevation of hepatic aminotransferases.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: Severe hepatotoxicity, elevated liver enzymes, potential liver failure
interaction_rules.warning_message: CRITICAL: Nevirapine + amodiaquine combination has caused severe liver toxicity. This combination should be avoided.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Use artemether-lumefantrine instead of AS-AQ in patients on nevirapine-based ART. Refer to HIV clinician before prescribing antimalarial.
references: Pfeil et al. 2012 — trial stopped due to hepatotoxicity

interaction_rules.mechanism: Lumefantrine moderately inhibits UGT1A1, the primary enzyme metabolising dolutegravir. This may increase dolutegravir exposure, though clinical significance is moderate.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: Possible increased dolutegravir side effects (nausea, headache, insomnia), generally manageable
interaction_rules.warning_message: MODERATE: Dolutegravir + artemether-lumefantrine may increase dolutegravir levels. Monitor for side effects. This combination is generally considered usable with monitoring.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Monitor for dolutegravir side effects. No dose adjustment typically required. Preferred over efavirenz + AL combination.

interaction_rules.mechanism: Rifampicin is the most potent known inducer of CYP450 enzymes and UGT1A1. It reduces dolutegravir plasma concentrations by approximately 75%, rendering standard doses non-therapeutic.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: HIV treatment failure, viral rebound, development of ARV resistance
interaction_rules.warning_message: CRITICAL: Rifampicin reduces dolutegravir blood levels by 75%. Standard dolutegravir dose WILL FAIL in TB/HIV co-infected patients.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Double dolutegravir dose to 50mg twice daily when co-administering with rifampicin. Consult HIV/TB co-infection clinician immediately. Do not attempt to manage this without specialist input.
alternatives: efavirenz-based regimen (EFV 600mg OD) is an alternative for TB/HIV co-infection per WHO guidelines

interaction_rules.mechanism: Rifampicin dramatically reduces protease inhibitor plasma levels through CYP3A4 induction. Boosted PI regimens cannot overcome this induction — levels remain subtherapeutic even with dose doubling.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: Complete loss of HIV viral suppression, resistance development, treatment failure
interaction_rules.warning_message: CRITICAL: Rifampicin + protease inhibitors is a contraindicated combination. HIV suppression will fail. This patient requires immediate regimen switch.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Switch HIV regimen away from PI-based therapy during TB treatment. WHO recommends efavirenz-based or dolutegravir-based (with double DTG dose) regimens for TB/HIV co-treatment.

interaction_rules.mechanism: Rifampicin strongly induces CYP2C9, the primary enzyme metabolising warfarin, reducing anticoagulant effect dramatically. INR can drop to subtherapeutic levels within days.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: Loss of anticoagulation, thromboembolic events (stroke, DVT, PE)
interaction_rules.warning_message: HIGH RISK: Rifampicin significantly reduces warfarin effectiveness. Patients may develop clots if not closely monitored.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Monitor INR weekly when starting or stopping rifampicin. Warfarin dose will likely need to be increased 2-5x. Consider low-molecular-weight heparin as alternative if INR monitoring unavailable.

interaction_rules.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit platelet aggregation and displace warfarin from plasma proteins, increasing free warfarin levels. Combined effect dramatically increases bleeding risk.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: Gastrointestinal bleeding, intracranial bleeding, prolonged bleeding
interaction_rules.warning_message: HIGH RISK: NSAIDs + anticoagulants significantly increase bleeding risk. This combination should be avoided.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Use paracetamol for pain/fever in anticoagulated patients. If NSAID is essential, use lowest dose + PPI cover and monitor INR closely.

interaction_rules.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandin synthesis, impairing the renal concentrating ability already compromised in sickle cell disease. Risk of renal papillary necrosis and acute kidney injury is elevated.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: Acute kidney injury, renal papillary necrosis, worsening of renal function
interaction_rules.warning_message: HIGH RISK: NSAIDs are dangerous in sickle cell disease patients due to high risk of kidney damage.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Use paracetamol or opioid analgesics for pain crisis management. Avoid NSAIDs. Ensure adequate hydration.
interaction_rules.note: Sickle cell disease has high prevalence across sub-Saharan Africa — this rule is a West African clinical priority.

interaction_rules.mechanism: Quinine stimulates insulin secretion from pancreatic beta cells. In diabetic patients on antidiabetics, this can cause additive hypoglycaemia, sometimes severe.
interaction_rules.warning_message: MODERATE: Quinine can cause low blood sugar in diabetic patients, especially when combined with diabetes medications.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Monitor blood glucose closely. Prefer artemisinin-based therapy over quinine where possible in diabetic patients.

interaction_rules.mechanism: Efavirenz was historically contraindicated in the first trimester due to teratogenicity concerns in animal studies. While evidence in humans is reassuring, WHO recommends dolutegravir as preferred ARV in pregnancy.
interaction_rules.clinical_consequence: Potential neural tube defects (animal data), though human risk appears low
interaction_rules.warning_message: HIGH CAUTION: Efavirenz in first trimester pregnancy. Current WHO guidelines recommend switching to dolutegravir-based regimen in pregnant women.
interaction_rules.recommendation: Refer to HIV/antenatal clinic. Switch to dolutegravir + TDF + 3TC per current WHO PMTCT guidelines.
